network.overall_strategy: This network follows the literature-supported conclusion that prebiotic chemistry robustly converges on racemic proline through multiple independent routes, while a fully demonstrated aqueous prebiotic route to homochiral L-proline remains unresolved. Accordingly, the network is built around four literature-grounded source families: (i) hydrothermal protometabolic production of alpha-ketoglutarate and glutamate feeding the glutamate -> glutamic-gamma-semialdehyde -> Delta1-pyrroline-5-carboxylate branch, (ii) surface cyanosulfidic photochemistry leading through a 4-carbon cyanohydrin, cyclic alpha-hydroxythioamide, gamma-butyrothiolactam, and proline aminonitrile, (iii) HCN oligomerization/polymer hydrolysis chemistry, and (iv) Miller-Urey spark-discharge occurrence of proline and glutamate. The final target node L-proline is reached only through a clearly marked speculative enantioenrichment/selection step because the supplied literature explicitly states that no validated prebiotic aqueous route to homochiral L-proline has been demonstrated. The network therefore maximizes literature fidelity, uses exact reported conditions where available, preserves mineral-agent requirements for Hydrothermal and Surface reactions, and makes the chirality bottleneck explicit rather than hiding it.

reactions.name: Hydrothermal protometabolic generation of alpha-ketoglutarate from CO2 under iron-sulfur catalysis

conditions.other: alkaline vent conditions, pH 9-11, serpentinization-derived H2; step represents network-level production rather than a single isolated bench-top transformation

reactions.mechanism: Iron-sulfur-driven CO2 fixation and protometabolic carboxylation/rearrangement chemistry yield TCA-like alpha-keto acid intermediates including alpha-ketoglutarate.
reactions.reasoning: Alpha-ketoglutarate is identified in the supplied literature as a central branch point and as accessible in protometabolic TCA-like chemistry driven by pyrite and related mineral systems. Muchowska et al. (2017) showed pyrite-driven production of many TCA intermediates including alpha-ketoglutarate, while Russell & Hall (1997, 2006) and Li et al. (2018) support iron-sulfur hydrothermal catalysis. Because the literature does not provide a single simple stoichiometric CO2->alpha-ketoglutarate step, this node is explicitly encoded as a network-level hydrothermal production process rather than an unjustified direct one-step reaction.

reactions.name: Nickel-catalyzed reductive amination of alpha-ketoglutarate to glutamate

conditions.catalyst: Ni catalyst in literature; connected here to hydrothermal reduced iron/magnetite milieu as mineral support
conditions.other: pH 8, anoxic; literature reports Ni/SiO2-Al2O3 or meteoritic Ni catalysts

reactions.mechanism: Alpha-keto acid undergoes reductive amination with ammonia under H2 to form the corresponding amino acid glutamate.
reactions.reasoning: Kaur, Moran et al. (2024) demonstrated reductive amination of keto acids to amino acids including glutamate at 22C, pH 8, and 1-10 bar H2 over nickel catalysts. Huber & Wachtershauser (1998, 2003) further support prebiotic reductive amination on sulfide-rich metal surfaces. This is one of the best-supported entry points into the glutamate-family pathway.

reactions.name: Speculative partial reduction of glutamate to glutamic-gamma-semialdehyde

conditions.other: alkaline reducing vent fluids with Fe2+/H2S; chemically demanding selective gamma-carboxyl reduction

reactions.mechanism: Selective reduction of the side-chain carboxyl group of glutamate affords the corresponding semialdehyde precursor GSA.
reactions.reasoning: The supplied literature explicitly identifies glutamate->GSA as the major unresolved bottleneck. The biological logic is established by Strecker (1957), and the prebiotic plausibility of reductive transformations in FeS/H2S systems is supported broadly by Huber & Wachtershauser (1998, 2003), but no definitive prebiotic demonstration of selective glutamate gamma-carboxyl reduction is cited. This reaction is therefore included only because it is necessary to connect the validated glutamate/P5C branch and is explicitly marked speculative, in direct accord with the literature's stated gap.

reactions.name: Spontaneous cyclization of glutamic-gamma-semialdehyde to Delta1-pyrroline-5-carboxylate

conditions.other: non-enzymatic aqueous solution; spontaneous intramolecular imine formation

reactions.mechanism: Intramolecular condensation between amino and aldehyde groups closes the five-membered ring to form the imine/carboxylate P5C.
reactions.reasoning: Vogel & Davis (1952) first characterized the spontaneous aqueous cyclization of glutamic-gamma-semialdehyde to Delta1-pyrroline-5-carboxylic acid. The supplied literature emphasizes this as one of the strongest and most prebiotically plausible steps in the whole proline network.

reactions.name: Reduction of Delta1-pyrroline-5-carboxylate to racemic proline

conditions.other: reducing serpentinization-fed vent fluids; Fe2+/FeS/H2 systems proposed

reactions.mechanism: Reductive hydrogenation of the cyclic imine in P5C yields proline without enantioselection.
reactions.reasoning: The supplied literature states that final reduction of P5C to proline could be driven by Fe2+, FeS/H2S systems, or molecular hydrogen from serpentinization. This step is therefore literature-consistent in environment and reductant class, though less directly bench-demonstrated than the cyanosulfidic route. Li et al. (2018) support greigite as a proto-enzymatic iron-sulfur catalyst in hydrothermal CO2/reduction chemistry.

reactions.name: HCN production in Miller-Urey volcanic spark-discharge mixtures

conditions.temperature: electrical discharge apparatus; variable local heating
conditions.catalyst: mineral shore concentration/support only; key energy source is spark discharge
conditions.other: CH4/N2/H2O with trace NH3 in anoxic atmosphere; volcanic-style discharge setting

reactions.mechanism: Electrical discharge through reduced gas mixtures generates nitriles including HCN, which dissolve into surface waters.
reactions.reasoning: Ring et al. (1972), Johnson et al. (2008), and Jiang et al. (2014) document amino-acid-yielding spark discharge chemistry in reduced volcanic gas mixtures. HCN is a standard feedstock arising in such systems and is necessary to connect the literature-supported HCN-based routes. Mineral agents are included to satisfy the required surface schema, but the key literature driver is electrical discharge rather than mineral catalysis.

reactions.name: HCN oligomerization to diaminomaleonitrile
reactions.mechanism: HCN undergoes concentration-dependent oligomerization to the tetramer DAMN.
reactions.reasoning: Oró (1961) established HCN oligomerization chemistry, and Ruiz-Bermejo et al. (2013) reviewed DAMN-centered prebiotic HCN polymer pathways. DAMN is a required intermediate if the HCN polymer branch is to be represented with appropriate mechanistic detail rather than an over-compressed direct HCN->proline step.

reactions.name: Hydrolysis and rearrangement of HCN polymer intermediates to a hydantoin-rich pool

conditions.other: complex HCN polymer hydrolysis; condition-sensitive product spectrum

reactions.mechanism: DAMN-containing HCN polymers hydrolyze and rearrange through mixed nitrile/amide intermediates to hydantoin-like amino acid precursor pools.
reactions.reasoning: Ruiz-Bermejo et al. (2013) reviewed 23 HCN polymer hydrolysis conditions and reported proline among amino acids detected under some higher-temperature conditions, with hydantoin-type intermediacy implicated in polymer hydrolysis chemistry. This step is intentionally represented as a mixed precursor pool to avoid the unjustified oversimplification criticized in previous attempts.

reactions.name: Release of racemic proline from hydantoin-rich HCN hydrolysis products

conditions.temperature: mild to moderate heating during continued hydrolysis
conditions.other: complex mixture hydrolysis; low-selectivity amino acid release

reactions.mechanism: Further hydrolysis of hydantoin-like and related nitrile-derived precursors releases amino acids including proline into a racemic pool.
reactions.reasoning: Ruiz-Bermejo et al. (2013) specifically note proline among amino acids obtained in some HCN polymer hydrolysis experiments. Because the literature supports occurrence but not a clean dedicated selective route, this reaction is encoded as release from a mixed precursor pool rather than an artificially precise direct conversion.

reactions.name: Cyanosulfidic formation of a 4-carbon cyanohydrin precursor

conditions.catalyst: Cu(I)/Cu(II) photoredox in literature; mineral support via sulfide and phosphate surfaces
conditions.other: neutral pH, anoxic, UV-irradiated shallow pools; Patel-Sutherland cyanosulfidic network

reactions.mechanism: HCN/H2S photoredox chemistry generates reduced and homologated nitrile-carbonyl intermediates culminating in a 4-carbon cyanohydrin precursor relevant to proline movement c.
reactions.reasoning: Patel et al. (2015) provide the strongest experimentally grounded route to prebiotic proline precursors and explicitly identify a 4-carbon cyanohydrin entering movement c. The supplied literature also specifies 35C, neutral pH, anoxic conditions, HCN + H2S + UV, and Cu photoredox cycling. This step is kept as a precursor-pool node because the report summarizes the class of intermediate without naming a single canonical structure.

reactions.name: Sulfur incorporation and cyclization of the 4-carbon cyanohydrin to cyclic alpha-hydroxythioamide

conditions.catalyst: Cu(I)/Cu(II) photoredox in literature; sulfide-rich mineral surfaces
conditions.other: neutral pH, anoxic, UV-active shallow pools

reactions.mechanism: Addition of sulfur to the 4-carbon cyanohydrin affords open-chain and cyclic alpha-hydroxythioamide species; the cyclic form is the ring-committing precursor.
reactions.reasoning: Patel et al. (2015) specifically report that the 4-carbon cyanohydrin reacts with H2S to give open-chain and cyclic alpha-hydroxythioamides en route to proline. The supplied literature emphasizes that the pyrrolidine framework emerges naturally from this geometry, making this a critical mechanistic step.

reactions.name: UV-driven conversion of cyclic alpha-hydroxythioamide to gamma-butyrothiolactam

conditions.catalyst: UV irradiation with Cu photoredox context from literature; TiO2 as photocatalytic surface
conditions.other: neutral pH, anoxic, sustained UV exposure

reactions.mechanism: Photochemical cyclization/deoxygenation of the cyclic alpha-hydroxythioamide yields gamma-butyrothiolactam.
reactions.reasoning: Patel et al. (2015) identify UV irradiation as the driving force for cyclization and deoxygenation to gamma-butyrothiolactam in the proline branch. This is the most direct literature-supported ring-forming route to proline precursors in the supplied report.

reactions.name: Photoreduction and cyanide addition to gamma-butyrothiolactam forming proline aminonitrile

conditions.catalyst: Cu(I)/Cu(II) photoredox in literature; phosphate-buffered shallow pools
conditions.other: neutral pH, anoxic, UV-driven reduction chemistry

reactions.mechanism: Gamma-butyrothiolactam undergoes photoreduction and HCN addition to generate the alpha-aminonitrile precursor of proline.
reactions.reasoning: Patel et al. (2015) explicitly report photoreduction and HCN addition from gamma-butyrothiolactam to the proline alpha-aminonitrile precursor (compound 47 in the paper). This is the strongest discrete precursor-to-proline route in the supplied literature.

reactions.name: Hydrolysis of proline aminonitrile to racemic proline

conditions.other: anoxic aqueous pools; hydrolysis of aminonitrile precursor

reactions.mechanism: Aminonitrile hydrolysis converts the nitrile-bearing proline precursor to the amino acid proline.
reactions.reasoning: Patel et al. (2015) identify hydrolysis of the proline aminonitrile as the terminal conversion to proline. As in classical Strecker-type chemistry, aminonitrile hydrolysis does not itself provide convincing homochiral selection, so the output is encoded as racemic proline.

reactions.name: Direct spark-discharge formation of racemic proline in complex amino acid mixtures

conditions.temperature: electrical discharge apparatus; variable local heating
conditions.catalyst: surface concentration on mineral-rich pools; key energy source is electric discharge
conditions.other: CH4/N2/H2O with trace NH3; volcanic spark conditions

reactions.mechanism: Electrical discharge through reduced gas mixtures forms a complex amino acid mixture containing proline as a minor but reproducible component.
reactions.reasoning: Ring et al. (1972) definitively identified proline in spark-discharge products; Johnson et al. (2008) and Jiang et al. (2014) reinforced broad amino acid formation in Miller-type archived systems. Parker et al. (2011) further noted that standard OPA/NAC methods miss proline because it is a secondary amine, implying underreporting. This reaction intentionally represents direct occurrence rather than a fictitious selective mechanism.

reactions.name: Direct spark-discharge formation of glutamate in complex amino acid mixtures

conditions.temperature: electrical discharge apparatus; variable local heating
conditions.catalyst: surface concentration on mineral-rich pools; key energy source is electric discharge
conditions.other: CH4/N2/H2O with trace NH3; complex product mixture

reactions.mechanism: Spark-discharge chemistry yields a broad amino acid distribution that includes glutamate/glutamic acid.
reactions.reasoning: The supplied literature notes glutamic acid as one of the abundant early amino acids in spark-discharge experiments and meteorites, with Ring et al. (1972), Johnson et al. (2008), and Jiang et al. (2014) supporting such product distributions. This node is included to create the literature-motivated convergence of surface and hydrothermal inputs onto the glutamate-family branch.

reactions.name: Speculative aqueous enantioenrichment/selection of L-proline from racemic proline

conditions.other: possible crystal-solution partitioning, mineral adsorption, or organocatalytic feedback; no validated prebiotic aqueous protocol

reactions.mechanism: Racemic proline pool is subjected to hypothesized symmetry-breaking and amplification processes, favoring the L-enantiomer under yet-unresolved prebiotic conditions.
reactions.reasoning: The supplied literature explicitly states that no demonstrated prebiotic aqueous route to homochiral L-proline exists. Hayashi et al. (2006) showed strong eutectic amplification for proline in CHCl3/EtOH, not prebiotic water; Hazen et al. (2001) support the principle of chiral mineral adsorption for other amino acids but not proline; Meinert et al. (2014, 2022) show small CPL-induced ee values; List et al. (2000) and Mathew et al. (2004) demonstrate proline's exceptional organocatalytic and autoinductive properties but not autocatalytic self-generation of L-proline. This reaction is therefore necessarily speculative and is included only because the requested target is L-proline.

pathways.name: Hydrothermal protometabolic glutamate-family route
pathways.description: Alpha-ketoglutarate formed in hydrothermal iron-sulfur protometabolism is reductively aminated to glutamate, then passes through the literature-emphasized GSA -> P5C -> proline branch before speculative L-selection.

pathways.name: Surface cyanosulfidic photochemistry route
pathways.description: Patel-Sutherland movement c from HCN/H2S under UV at 35C through a 4-carbon cyanohydrin, cyclic alpha-hydroxythioamide, gamma-butyrothiolactam, and proline aminonitrile to racemic proline, then speculative L-selection.

pathways.name: Spark-fed cyanosulfidic route
pathways.description: Miller-Urey style discharge provides HCN feedstock that is then routed into the Patel-Sutherland cyanosulfidic proline branch.

pathways.name: HCN polymer hydrolysis route
pathways.description: HCN oligomerizes to DAMN, proceeds through hydantoin-rich hydrolysis intermediates, and releases racemic proline in complex mixtures before speculative L-selection.

pathways.name: Spark-fed HCN polymer route
pathways.description: Spark-discharge chemistry supplies HCN to the DAMN/polymer hydrolysis branch, adding an atmospheric-surface source of racemic proline.

pathways.name: Direct Miller-Urey proline occurrence route
pathways.description: Proline appears directly as a minor product in spark-discharge mixtures and is then subjected to speculative L-enrichment.

pathways.name: Spark-derived glutamate into the glutamate-family branch
pathways.description: Surface spark chemistry supplies glutamate, which enters the GSA -> P5C -> proline branch without requiring hydrothermal alpha-ketoglutarate generation.

pathways.name: Hydrothermal-to-surface crossover via shared HCN/proline pool convergence
pathways.description: Independent hydrothermal glutamate-family synthesis and surface HCN chemistry converge on the shared racemic proline hub, emphasizing network redundancy before speculative L-selection.

pathways.name: Surface dual-source convergence on racemic proline
pathways.description: The cyanosulfidic branch and the HCN polymer branch independently generate the same racemic proline hub, which is then subjected to speculative L-selection.

pathways.name: Glutamate-hub convergence route
pathways.description: Glutamate is produced both hydrothermally and by spark discharge, converging on the same downstream GSA -> P5C -> proline sequence before speculative L-selection.
